name: skill_wager
description: Tests the interaction between online skill learning and deposit-driven effective wagers across agents with different forecast quality.

name: forecast_aggregation
description: Compares aggregate forecast quality across weighting rules: uniform, deposit-only, skill-only, and the full mechanism.

name: behaviour_matrix
description: Holds the mechanism fixed and varies only behaviour modules: benign baselines, realistic frictions, and adversaries.

name: parameter_sweep
description: Sweeps over λ and σ_min to map the Pareto frontier between forecast quality and concentration.

name: calibration
description: Evaluates the calibration of the aggregate forecast by checking quantile coverage against nominal levels.

name: settlement_sanity
description: Verifies that the settlement mechanism is budget-balanced and payouts are non-negative.

name: fixed_deposit
description: Examines skill evolution when all agents make identical fixed deposits each round.

name: intermittency_stress_test
description: Compares IID vs bursty vs edge-threshold vs avoid-skill-decay participation.

name: sybil
description: Tests whether sybil splitting gains an advantage under the mechanism across three scenarios: identical, diversified, and strategic deposit splitting.

name: arbitrage_scan
description: Runs arbitrageurs across a parameter grid to see when arbitrage appears and whether it dominates wealth.

name: weight_learning
description: Tests whether online learning converges to the correct weights for a mixture DGP.

name: skill_recovery
description: Tests whether skill estimates recover after perturbation across CRPS and MAE scoring.

name: detection_adaptation
description: Compares a fixed manipulator with an adaptive evader that balances manipulation with detector evasion.

name: preference_stress_test
description: Compares truthful vs hedged risk-averse reporting to evaluate bias and welfare.

name: deposit_policy_comparison
description: Compares deposit policies to understand their effect on market concentration and forecast quality.

name: collusion_stress
description: Three-member coalition broadcasting the wager-weighted mean / median of member beliefs. Tests whether Chun-Shachter (2011) coalition attack on WSWMs extracts positive profit under the MAE mechanism.

name: informed_collusion
description: Chun-Shachter coalition with per-member privileged lagged information on an AR(1) DGP. Combines two attack vectors: coalition arbitrage from residual member disagreement and the insider precision bonus.

name: insider_advantage
description: Compares no insider vs F_{t-1}-compliant lagged insider vs outcome-leaking attacker (allow_leakage=True audit) on an AR(1) DGP.

name: wash_activity_gaming
description: Multi-account activity gaming with two styles: anchor (low score-rule cost) and split_bet (maximal internal transfer). Measures inflation and wash-account profit.

name: strategic_reporting
description: Sweep over pull and target to measure aggregate shift vs attacker profit. Reveals a non-monotone frontier: gentle pulls are both profitable and most effective at shifting r_hat.

name: sybil_arbitrage
description: Fans the Chen-Devanur arbitrage attack across k sybil accounts with equal total stake. Checks Lambert sybilproofness holds on top of an already-profitable attack.

name: arbitrage_crowd_size
description: 2D sweep of the arbitrage attack over lambda and n_benign. Shows attacker profit scales roughly linearly with crowd size because disagreement grows.

name: reputation_reset
description: Whitewashing attacker (Feldman-Chuang 2004) that abandons a collapsed identity and rejoins as a newcomer. Compares fixed identity vs reset; measures how much the mechanism's staleness decay blunts the attack.

name: identity_attack_matrix
description: Compares single account, sybil split, reputation reset, and collusive multi-account identity policies.

name: drift_adaptation
description: Compares baseline, fast-adaptor, and slow-adaptor belief policies against a drifting DGP.

name: stake_policy_matrix
description: Compares fixed-fraction, Kelly-like, house-money, lumpy-tier, break-even, and volatility-sensitive staking policies.
